# Integration test for MCP tools: mcpspy, mcp-shadow, mcpcat, mcpdiff, and mcp-replay
# This test demonstrates the comprehensive workflow of using these tools together

# Start by creating a test server command
echo '#!/bin/bash' > test_server.sh
echo 'echo "Server started" >&2' >> test_server.sh
echo 'while IFS= read -r line; do' >> test_server.sh
echo '  request=$(echo "$line" | jq -r .method 2>/dev/null || echo "")' >> test_server.sh
echo '  id=$(echo "$line" | jq -r .id 2>/dev/null || echo "")' >> test_server.sh
echo '  if [[ "$request" == "initialize" ]]; then' >> test_server.sh
echo '    echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":$id,\"result\":{\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"test-server\",\"version\":\"v1\"},\"capabilities\":{}}}"' >> test_server.sh
echo '  elif [[ "$request" == "ping" ]]; then' >> test_server.sh
echo '    echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":$id,\"result\":{\"message\":\"pong\",\"timestamp\":$(date +%s)}}"' >> test_server.sh
echo '  elif [[ "$request" == "tools/list" ]]; then' >> test_server.sh
echo '    echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":$id,\"result\":[{\"name\":\"echo\",\"description\":\"Echo tool\"}]}"' >> test_server.sh
echo '  fi' >> test_server.sh
echo 'done' >> test_server.sh
chmod +x test_server.sh

# Create an alternative server for shadow testing (v2)
echo '#!/bin/bash' > test_server_v2.sh
echo 'echo "Server v2 started" >&2' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo 'while IFS= read -r line; do' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '  request=$(echo "$line" | jq -r .method 2>/dev/null || echo "")' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '  id=$(echo "$line" | jq -r .id 2>/dev/null || echo "")' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '  if [[ "$request" == "initialize" ]]; then' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '    echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":$id,\"result\":{\"serverInfo\":{\"name\":\"test-server\",\"version\":\"v2\"},\"capabilities\":{\"tools\":true}}}"' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '  elif [[ "$request" == "ping" ]]; then' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '    echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":$id,\"result\":{\"message\":\"pong from v2\",\"timestamp\":$(date +%s),\"extra\":\"v2 feature\"}}"' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '  elif [[ "$request" == "tools/list" ]]; then' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '    echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"id\":$id,\"result\":[{\"name\":\"echo\",\"description\":\"Echo tool v2\"},{\"name\":\"calc\",\"description\":\"Calculator tool\"}]}"' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo '  fi' >> test_server_v2.sh
echo 'done' >> test_server_v2.sh
chmod +x test_server_v2.sh

# Test 1: Use mcpspy to monitor and record MCP traffic
exec mcpspy -v -f trace1.mcp -- ./test_server.sh 2>/dev/null &
sleep 1

# Send some test requests
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1}' | nc localhost 7000 > response1.json 2>/dev/null
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","params":{},"id":2}' | nc localhost 7000 > response2.json 2>/dev/null
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":3}' | nc localhost 7000 > response3.json 2>/dev/null

# Stop mcpspy
pkill -f mcpspy > /dev/null 2>&1
sleep 1

# Test 2: Use mcp-shadow to compare two server implementations
exec mcp-shadow --primary "./test_server.sh" --shadow "./test_server_v2.sh" --compare -o shadow_trace.mcp << EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","params":{},"id":2}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/list","params":{},"id":3}
EOF

# Test 3: Use mcpcat to colorize and view the traces
# This would normally show colored output but we'll just verify it works
exec mcpcat trace1.mcp > colorized1.txt
exec mcpcat -color=never shadow_trace.mcp > colorized2.txt

# Verify mcpcat processed the files
grep -q 'mcp-recv' colorized1.txt
grep -q 'mcp-send' colorized1.txt
grep -q 'mcp-send-shadow' colorized2.txt

# Test 4: Use mcpdiff to compare the traces
exec mcpdiff -t -s trace1.mcp shadow_trace.mcp > diff_output.txt

# Verify mcpdiff found differences
grep -q 'version' diff_output.txt

# Test 5: Use mcpdiff in compare mode for shadow trace
exec mcpdiff -compare -s shadow_trace.mcp > shadow_diff.txt

# Verify compare mode found differences between primary and shadow
grep -q 'v2' shadow_diff.txt

# Test 6: Use mcp-replay to replay recorded sessions
# Create a simple mock client script
echo '#!/bin/bash' > mock_client.sh
echo 'echo "Client ready" >&2' >> mock_client.sh
echo 'while IFS= read -r line; do' >> mock_client.sh
echo '  echo "Received: $line" >&2' >> mock_client.sh
echo '  if echo "$line" | grep -q "initialize"; then' >> mock_client.sh
echo '    echo "{\"jsonrpc\":\"2.0\",\"method\":\"ping\",\"params\":{},\"id\":10}"' >> mock_client.sh
echo '  fi' >> mock_client.sh
echo 'done' >> mock_client.sh
chmod +x mock_client.sh

# Replay as mock server
exec mcp-replay -mock-server -f trace1.mcp -o replay_output.mcp &
sleep 1

# Send a request to the mock server
echo '{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":1}' | nc localhost 8080 > mock_response.json 2>/dev/null

# Stop the mock server
pkill -f mcp-replay > /dev/null 2>&1

# Test 7: Use mcp-sort to normalize timestamps for comparison
exec mcp-sort -strip trace1.mcp > trace1_normalized.mcp
exec mcp-sort -strip shadow_trace.mcp > shadow_trace_normalized.mcp

# Compare normalized traces
exec mcpdiff trace1_normalized.mcp shadow_trace_normalized.mcp > normalized_diff.txt

# Test 8: Chain tools together in a pipeline
# mcpspy to record -> mcpcat to view -> mcp-sort to normalize -> mcpdiff to compare
exec mcpspy -f pipeline.mcp -- ./test_server.sh << EOF | mcpcat -color=never | tee pipeline_view.txt
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{},"id":100}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"ping","params":{},"id":101}
EOF

# Clean up temporary files
rm -f test_server.sh test_server_v2.sh mock_client.sh

# Verify all tools produced output
test -s trace1.mcp
test -s shadow_trace.mcp
test -s colorized1.txt
test -s colorized2.txt
test -s diff_output.txt
test -s shadow_diff.txt
test -s replay_output.mcp
test -s trace1_normalized.mcp
test -s shadow_trace_normalized.mcp
test -s normalized_diff.txt
test -s pipeline.mcp
test -s pipeline_view.txt

echo "All MCP tools integration tests passed!"
